blast_radius: Python trainer only (train.py, run_loop.py) + new driver/tests; Kotlin bridge + ONNX contract byte-unchanged.
council: intake 34 triaged; plan-review 34 triaged → 4 revisions applied (--continual flag, byte-verbatim else-branch, collapsed resume API, RESULTS round-8 disentangling); APPROVE w/ revisions

review_focus.item: Micro-batch size-weighting (n_c/N) + shared _ac_loss_terms helper == whole-batch within fp tol
review_focus.why: AC6 numerical equivalence; wrong weighting silently biases the gradient

review_focus.item: Divergence guard restores opt.state_dict() too, not just net weights
review_focus.why: a diverged round would poison the persistent Adam moments

review_focus.item: Warm rounds skip manual_seed; continual is default-on with no toggle
review_focus.why: re-seeding would re-init weights; determinism depends on round-0-only seeding

review_focus.item: Resume requires curve.csv + ckpt + opt sidecar, fail-fast, weights_only=True
review_focus.why: multi-artifact consistency; no silent fresh optimizer; no pickle RCE

review_focus.item: Whole-batch path byte-identical when micro_batch_steps is 0/>=N (apples-to-apples small-rung arm)
review_focus.why: AC1 vs v4 23.0% requires only the regime to change

top_decisions.decision: In-process (net,opt) carryover gated by --continual flag (default True)
Inject optimizer constructed once alongside the net
Whole-batch path byte-verbatim in else-branch; chunk path new code; equivalence tested
top_decisions.rejected: shared loss helper (conflicts byte-verbatim freeze; plan-council FND-0002)

top_decisions.decision: Divergence guard restores net AND optimizer state
Resume API collapsed to (net,opt); run_loop does disk-load, fail-fast, weights_only=True
top_decisions.rejected: 4 nullable trainer params (plan-council FND-0001); pickle-trusting load

risks.text: Micro-batch path subtly diverges from whole-batch (wrong weighting / fp drift)
risks.mitigation: shared _ac_loss_terms; test_microbatch.py asserts loss+grad-norm+weights match within atol≈1e-5; small-rung arm uses no-op (byte-identical)

risks.text: Continual training diverges/collapses mid-run, wasting hours of compute
risks.mitigation: opt-aware divergence guard rolls back net+opt to last-good; diverged flag in curve.csv; babysat run; --resume restart-safe

risks.text: Warm-start still doesn't clear blind (28.9%) — the regime wasn't the bottleneck either
risks.mitigation: this is a legitimate experiment outcome; AC2 reports the result PLAINLY either way with z/p; the curve over 16 rounds shows the trajectory

rollback: git revert the two trainer commits; ckpt/opt sidecars + run dirs are under runs/ (gitignored), delete to reclaim disk. Contract unchanged so no model regen needed.

change_inventory.why: inject optimizer, opt-aware guard, _ac_loss_terms helper, micro-batch path, warm/resume in 3 trainers
carry (net,opt) across loop, atomic ckpt+opt saves, resume net+opt fail-fast, --micro-batch-steps, metrics fields
two-arm experiment driver (small + medium rung), ceiling evals, bench-onnx

verification: pytest python/tests/test_microbatch.py — micro-batch == whole-batch within atol
pytest python/tests/test_continual_resume.py — resume equivalence + AC6 parity
pytest python/tests — full suite green (no regression)
python -m unciv_train.analyze --root runs/structured-Medium --ceiling-games 200 --eval-seed 4242424 — z-test vs v4 23.0% and vs blind 28.9%
out_of_scope: Cross-round replay buffer (DEFERRED — needs behavior-logp recording first)
